title: Very long compile and run times on a matrix multiplication algorithm
body:
In order to test the multi-threaded simulation capabilities I wrote a very parallel matrix multiplication algorithm.

Assuming that an NxN matrix multiplication is being performed, the RTL consists of the following:

4 N - register files. Each register file containing N registers.

An N/2 dot product unit that performs 2N multiplications in one clock cycle. I.E. One element of the output matrix is computed in 2 clock cycles.

A control unit which controls the reading and writing of the register files as well as the execution of the matrix multiplication.

All of these instances are instantiated using generate statements.

The matrix multiplication takes N squared (instead of cubed) clock cycles to do the multiplication.

This ticked is being filed for the following two reasons:

C++ (not verilator) compile times are too long. It seems that it generates a separate compilation for each generate statement. Compilation times above 64x64 are prohibitive.

Although for small size matrices verilator is much faster than commercial simulators (cannot disclose the numbers because of contractual reasons with EDA vendors) verilator becomes very slow for large matrices (above 64x64).

I would also like to learn how I can enable multi-threading in the simulation in verilator since this is a highly parallel design and I am running it into a 22 core machine.

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

Yes. Although I don't see an attach button.

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
 
Verilator 4.211 devel rev v4.210-21-g17cc452f 

Would you be willing to try to fix Verilator yourself with assistance?

Yes. Although this problem might be beyond my simulator knowledge

To run a testcase please do the following (once I can attache the test case):

cd to <your location>/matrix_multiplier_N/tools/verilator
edit the matrix_multiplier_default_compile_verilator_24.csh  matrix_multiplier_default_run_verilator_24.csh
scripts. And change the location to the location of the root directory of the test case.

Run the two scripts runs verilator and compiles the generated c code.
The other runs the matrix multiplication with a random number seeded by the xx file.

Thanks
Sergio

Note: The size of the matrix canb e changed by the -DSIZE=<size>  define. A word size of 32 would fit reasonable sized matrices. The multiplication is done using positive integers.
